rationale: The subject home within the orange bounding box is a multi-gabled townhome-style unit with a dark charcoal/gray asphalt shingle roof. Across all visible roof planes — the main north-facing slope, the central ridge area, and the smaller gable sections — the shingle color is uniform and consistent with no distinct discoloration patches, color mismatches, or staining tracks visible. The ridge lines appear geometrically straight with no visible sag or deviation compared to the neighboring identical units on either side. No tarps, foreign debris, or large missing material sections are detectable. There is partial tree canopy encroachment along the south/southeast lower edge of the bounding box, which slightly limits assessment of that lower eave zone; however, the area visible there shows no anomalies. All visible roof surfaces compare favorably in texture and color to the immediately adjacent units, which appear to be the same age and construction, supporting a no-issue determination. No findings met the confidence threshold for inclusion.
